## Tier
2 — KDS feature polish in `woosoo-nexus`. No order-state machine, POS parity, or contract change.

## Branch
`agent/kds-action-payload-optimistic` (woosoo-nexus) · specialist commit `ac32325` · PR #197

## Problem
KDS `advance` / `recall` / `toggleItem` endpoints returned only `{ status }` (or `{ done, done_at }` for toggle). The board only mutated when the Echo broadcast arrived. If Reverb was slow or briefly down, the operator clicked Mark Ready / Recall and saw no immediate UI change — the action LOOKED unresponsive even though the server had committed it.

This is the broadcast-down resilience gap from the kds-implementation-plan P4 phase. The simplest, smallest fix is to make the action's HTTP response carry the same payload the broadcast carries, so the client can apply it immediately and let Echo reconcile idempotently.

## Success Criterion
- `advance` and `recall` responses include `order` (full `OrderBroadcastPayload::make()` shape).
- `toggleItem` response includes `item_id` and `order_id` alongside `done` / `done_at` (matches the ItemToggled broadcast shape).
- `Display.vue` dispatches `board.applyOrderUpdate(response.order)` / `board.applyItemToggle(response)` from the HTTP response.
- All existing tests stay green; new tests lock the response contract.
- `npm run typecheck`, `npm run lint:check`, `npm run build`, `npm run test:unit`, `php artisan test`, `vendor/bin/pint --dirty --format agent` all pass.

## Contrarian Review
Tier 2; one app (`woosoo-nexus`); additive response shape, no state machine touch. Simpler paths considered and rejected:
- Optimistic mutation client-side from old ticket state (e.g. local `state = next`) — requires duplicating server transition logic on the client; fragile.
- Polling fallback — adds noise + load; broadcast IS the right channel, we just want the action to be self-sufficient if a broadcast is delayed.

## Specialist Investigation & Implementation

### Files changed (4)
- `woosoo-nexus/app/Http/Controllers/Admin/KdsController.php` — import `OrderBroadcastPayload`; extend `advance()` / `recall()` JSON responses with `order`; extend `toggleItem()` with `item_id` + `order_id`.
- `woosoo-nexus/resources/js/components/KDS/kdsApi.ts` — `KdsActionResponse` and `KdsToggleResponse` types; updated return types on three functions.
- `woosoo-nexus/resources/js/pages/KDS/Display.vue` — `advanceTicket`, `recallTicket`, `toggleItem` dispatch `board.applyOrderUpdate` / `applyItemToggle` from the HTTP response.
- `woosoo-nexus/tests/Feature/Admin/KdsControllerTest.php` — +3 tests: `advance response carries the full broadcast payload`, `recall response carries the full broadcast payload`, `toggle response carries item_id and order_id for optimistic apply`.

### Idempotency note
`useKdsBoard.applyOrderUpdate` replaces by id (`findIndex` then `map`/`push`); `applyItemToggle` replaces the item by id. The Echo broadcast landing milliseconds after the HTTP response carries the same state; second apply is a no-op render-wise.

## Code Simplification
No simplifier-eligible duplication introduced. The new return paths reuse `OrderBroadcastPayload::make()` (single source of truth for the wire shape).
